tasks.title: Traceboard Incident Timeline Landing Page
tasks.topic: Build a polished one-page landing page for an incident timeline debugger called Traceboard.
tasks.prompt: Build a polished one-page landing page for Traceboard, an incident timeline debugger for engineering teams. The first viewport should make the product and audience clear. Include navigation, a strong hero, feature sections, a workflow section, accessible calls to action, and responsive styling. Use a real app-like visual or generated asset instead of decorative placeholder boxes. Keep the page usable as a static Vite React app.
successCriteria: The page renders without console errors on desktop and mobile.
The first viewport clearly communicates Traceboard and incident timeline debugging.
Navigation and calls to action are keyboard accessible.
The visual design is polished, dense enough for technical buyers, and avoids generic template patterns.
Responsive layout has no horizontal overflow at 390px width.

tasks.title: Civic Slot Public-Service Landing Page
tasks.topic: Build an accessible public-service landing page for appointment booking called Civic Slot.
tasks.prompt: Build an accessible public-service landing page for Civic Slot, an appointment booking service for local government offices. Prioritize clarity, trust, readable forms, visible focus states, and plain language. Include a booking-oriented hero, service categories, availability explanation, accessibility commitments, and a simple contact or support section. Keep it static and responsive.
successCriteria: The page uses semantic landmarks and visible focus styles.
Copy is plain, public-service appropriate, and avoids marketing exaggeration.
Form controls or booking controls have labels, helper text, and sufficient contrast.
The page renders cleanly on mobile and desktop.
No console errors are emitted.

tasks.title: Arcwell Modular Desk Lamp Landing Page
tasks.topic: Build a premium product landing page for a modular desk lamp called Arcwell.
tasks.prompt: Build a premium product landing page for Arcwell, a modular desk lamp with swappable arms, lenses, and base finishes. The page should feel like a refined product site with a strong first-viewport product signal, product imagery or a generated product visual, concise specifications, configuration controls, and a buying call to action. Avoid beige craft defaults and generic feature-card layouts.
successCriteria: The first viewport clearly shows or evokes the Arcwell lamp.
Configuration or product-detail controls are understandable and accessible.
The palette and typography feel premium without generic beige and brass styling.
Responsive layout has no text overlap or horizontal overflow.
Build and runtime checks pass.

tasks.title: Interactive Canvas Orbit Field
tasks.topic: Build an interactive canvas orbit field with pointer response and pause controls.
tasks.prompt: Build an interactive canvas orbit field. Particles or satellites should orbit smoothly, respond to pointer movement, and expose a visible pause or resume control. The animation must honor prefers-reduced-motion by reducing or stopping automatic movement. Keep controls accessible and keep the canvas crisp across viewport sizes.
successCriteria: A canvas or equivalent animated surface renders nonblank pixels.
Frames differ over time when motion is allowed.
Pointer movement visibly affects the field.
A pause or resume control stops and restarts motion.
Reduced-motion users receive a static or heavily reduced animation.

tasks.title: Scroll-Driven Assembly-Line Animation
tasks.topic: Build a scroll-driven assembly-line animation with reduced-motion fallback.
tasks.prompt: Build a scroll-driven browser animation that shows an assembly-line machine moving parts through stations. Scroll should drive meaningful progress through the sequence, not just reveal static sections. Include a reduced-motion fallback that presents the same information without scroll hijacking or continuous motion.
successCriteria: The page has a visible animated machine or assembly sequence.
Scroll position changes the scene state or progress.
Reduced-motion mode keeps the content usable without forced motion.
No scroll-linked React state loops or console errors.
Mobile layout remains readable.

tasks.title: Metrics Data Rain Animation
tasks.topic: Build a browser animation that turns incoming metrics into falling, colliding bars.
tasks.prompt: Build a browser animation that turns incoming metric events into falling bars that collide or stack. Include visible metric labels, a live or simulated feed, pause controls, and a reduced-motion mode. The animation should show measurable frame changes over time and should not rely on inaccessible color alone.
successCriteria: Incoming metrics are represented as falling or colliding bars.
Frames differ over time while running.
Pause or resume controls work.
Reduced-motion mode prevents continuous falling motion.
Metric meaning is conveyed with labels or shapes as well as color.

tasks.title: Typed LRU Cache With TTL
tasks.topic: Implement a typed LRU cache with TTL, eviction stats, and tests.
tasks.prompt: Implement a typed LRU cache with TTL support. It should support get, set, has, delete, clear, size, max size enforcement, least-recently-used eviction, per-entry TTL expiration, and stats for hits, misses, evictions, and expired entries. Add focused tests for recency refresh, TTL behavior, updates, deletes, and capacity eviction.
successCriteria: The cache is generic over key and value types.
Least-recently-used eviction is deterministic.
Expired entries are not returned and are counted.
Stats are accurate across hits, misses, evictions, deletes, and clear.
TypeScript and test checks pass.

tasks.title: Interval Coalescer
tasks.topic: Fix and extend an interval coalescer with edge-case tests.
tasks.prompt: Fix and extend an interval coalescer. It should normalize reversed endpoints, reject invalid numbers, preserve deterministic ordering, merge overlapping intervals, optionally merge touching intervals, and handle empty and single-item inputs. Add edge-case tests for negative ranges, duplicates, touching ranges, reversed endpoints, and invalid values.
successCriteria: Overlapping intervals merge correctly.
Touching interval behavior is configurable.
Invalid intervals produce useful errors.
Input order does not affect output.
TypeScript and test checks pass.

tasks.title: Topological Dependency Batches
tasks.topic: Implement topological batching with cycle diagnostics and deterministic ordering.
tasks.prompt: Implement topological batching for dependency graphs. Return an array of batches where each batch can run in parallel after prior batches complete. Include deterministic alphabetical ordering, include dependencies that are only referenced but not declared, and throw a diagnostic cycle error that names the cycle members. Add tests for diamonds, disconnected graphs, missing dependency declarations, and cycles.
successCriteria: Batches obey dependency ordering.
Nodes within each batch are deterministic.
Referenced-only dependencies are included.
Cycles throw structured diagnostic errors.
TypeScript and test checks pass.
